prompt: Explain the water cycle, in prose, without using any lists, bullets, headings, or numbered steps. Keep it to one paragraph.
response: Water rises unnoticed from oceans, lakes, and the damp breath of leaves, drifting skyward as vapor that cools into soft, advancing clouds; when the clouds grow heavy with gathered memory they release their burden as rain or snow that murmurs onto rooftops, trails through rivers of asphalt, and seeps into the patient ground, slipping past roots and stones until it joins the quiet flow of underground streams or rejoins the waiting arms of rivers that carry it back toward the open sea, where the sun again begins the gentle task of lifting it skyward, an endless turning of breath and fall that has no beginning or end, only the slow pulse of the planet remembering itself.
